name: Florida
tooltip: Florida — Cat 2: Section 540.08 plus deepfake civil action. 40-year post-mortem. Strong NCII enforcement.
rightOfPublicity: Yes — Fla. Stat. § 540.08. Covers unauthorized commercial use of name, portrait, photograph, or other likeness for purposes of trade or commercial advertising. Living and deceased persons.
postMortem: Yes — 40 years after death. Fla. Stat. § 540.08(5). The right is descendible.
aiCoverage: Yes. Fla. Stat. § 836.13 (altered sexual depiction; SB 1798, 2022) creates a civil action for AI-altered sexual images with a $10,000 statutory damages floor. Fla. Stat. § 106.145 (HB 919, 2024) covers political AI disclosure. Brooke's Law (HB 1161, effective June 10, 2025) further expands § 836.13 enforcement.
remedy: Civil suit under § 540.08 for general likeness misuse. Civil suit under § 836.13 for altered sexual content with $10,000 statutory floor. Criminal referral for political deepfake violations.
whatsInteresting: Florida's $10,000 statutory floor for altered sexual depictions is one of the highest in the country, and Brooke's Law in 2025 added stronger enforcement teeth. If you're a creator whose likeness gets used in AI-generated explicit content, Florida is one of the cleaner states to file in. The state's 40-year post-mortem term plus active production-incentive program also means a lot of estate planning for entertainers passes through Florida domicile considerations. In 2026, Florida tried twice to pass an AI Bill of Rights (SB 482 / HB 1395 in the regular session, SB 2D in special session) that would have banned commercial AI-generated use of name, portrait, photo, image, or likeness without express consent, with $10,000+ DLA fines. The Senate passed both versions 37-1, but the House never took up SB 482 (died in messages March 13, 2026), and Speaker Daniel Perez announced on April 28 that the special session would address only redistricting — killing SB 2D the same day. Florida's AI Bill of Rights is dead for 2026 absent another special session. The existing § 540.08 framework and 2024 deepfake/election infrastructure remain intact, but the state's forward-looking position on AI likeness rights has materially softened.
primarySources: SB 482 / HB 1395 (Brodeur, 'AI Bill of Rights,' regular session — died March 13, 2026) — [flsenate.gov](http://flsenate.gov)
SB 2D (Brodeur, 'AI Bill of Rights,' special session — died April 28, 2026) — [flsenate.gov](http://flsenate.gov)
